headline: Set clear message statuses for sources
subtext: Recipients can decide what sources see at their temporary reply address after a tip comes in.
image_alt_text: Social graphic showing a Hush Line desktop settings screen with the Message Statuses section open. The page explains that senders can check message status at a temporary address and shows an editable Waiting status text box with an Update Reply Text button.

social.bluesky:
When a source checks back after sending a tip, silence creates confusion.

Hush Line lets recipients set the status text shown at a temporary reply address, so people can see where a message stands without turning disclosures into live chat.

Sign up at https://hushline.app.
social.linkedin:
When a source checks back after sending a tip, silence can create confusion.

Hush Line lets recipients set the status text shown at the source's temporary reply address, so a newsroom, law office, or other intake team can show where a message stands without turning the workflow into live chat.

Sign up at https://hushline.app.
social.mastodon:
When someone checks back after sending a tip, they should not be left guessing.

Hush Line lets recipients set the status text shown at the source's temporary reply address, so people can see where a message stands without turning disclosures into live chat.

Sign up at https://hushline.app.

rationale: Recent daily posts were already heavy on directory and onboarding themes, so this avoids another repeat while still staying close to documented user needs. The screen shows a concrete recipient workflow tied to Hush Line's one-way disclosure model and recent message-handling work, and it is a strong production pick because it is above the fold, light theme, desktop, and clearly non-admin.
copy_brief: Write for recipients and staff using Hush Line day to day, not for platform admins.

matched_pull_requests.title: #1802 Allow Admins to Suspend an Account, Blocking Message Submission

title: Settings - Message Statuses (artvandelay)
